description: Presbytery approved a TE for reception into the Presbytery, who took exception to the standards "with regard to the Christian Sabbath by denying the Biblical injunction to observe the Sabbath by resting from our worldly employments and recreations, and by denying that the Sabbath was established by God at the beginning of the world as a commandment binding upon all men in all ages." (minutes of October 25, 1997, page 1, lines 19-22). The TE's exception completely denies the entire concept of the sabbath as defined in exodus 20:8-11, WCF XXI 7-8; WLC 116-121; WSC 59-62.
There is a reference to a previous called meeting for which we have no minutes
response: Minutes were sent in and examined.

description: Omissions to parts of ordination exams; no mention of church, session, or endorsement for any candidate; more than one candidate's ordination exam omits languages and PCA history (BCO 21-4 and RAO 14-3.e.5, BCO 18-2; RAO 14-6.c.2). These exceptions are repeated from last year.
That exams for ordinations & transfer do not list all parts.
response: We acknowledge that we erred in not recording all the parts as having been addressed. We will attempt to be sure we do so in the future.

description: No record of required 3/4 vote for waiving internship requirements
response: We acknowledge the lack of record. A search does not reveal any notation about the vote waiver. We will attempt to be sure to record waivers in the future.

description: (P. 3, "Examining," Paragraph 2): Reference to an examination in previous meeting is not found in the minutes of the previous meeting.
response: We acknowledge the lack of record of the examination. A review of the notes does reflect the particulars of the exam. We will continue to make every effort to keep more accurate records than this incident reflects.

description: No record of examination in history of the Presbyterian Church in America
response: "The church history examination mentioned in paragraph 76-20.2 of the February 13, 1996, stated meeting of covenant presbytery included an examination in the history of the PCA. We regret this was not specifically stated in the minutes, and we will be more careful in the future."

description: Teaching elder removed without censure by administrative act of presbytery when judicial action seems necessary.
response: Presbytery respectfully acknowledges the exception taken, for future reference, but does not see that remedial action is possible in this situation.

description: Presbytery approved mission church elders' relaxed view of the sabbath as "within the bounds of current acceptable PCA interpretation and practice." This statement appears to imply that this view is in conformity with WCF 21.8., rather than an allowable exception.
response: Eastern Carolina Presbytery agrees that the motion was poorly worded. The commission did not mean to state that the PCA has a 'current interpretation' of WCF22.8. ECP agrees that the motion should have been worded, "... grant the exception of these elders to WCF22.8 as being not 'fundamental', as used in the second Elder vow (BCO 24-5-2). Such exceptions are within current PCA practice. These men believe....". This response has been adopted by ECP, meeting on October 18, 1997, and a notation has been added to the April 1996 Minutes referring to this action.

description: Minutes not received.
Candidate not asked BCO questions for coming under care
response: He was examined for coming under care of Presbytery and we apologize for failing to record the action of the questions of BCO 18:3 by the moderator and his answering them in the affirmative. He was then examined for licensure as recorded in the minutes of september 24, 1996.

description: Session recommendation of candidate is waived
response: Mr. Andrew Southwell had been a member of the Kirk of the Hills Presbyterian Church from 1990 to 1994. He then served at the chesterfield presbyterian church of St. Louis from 1993-1994 He then went to Oxford, England for two years for advanced studies. It was from there that he came to the Southwood Presbyterian Church in Huntsville, Alabama. because of the above events, the Presbytery unanimously adopted a motion to wave the six month membership requirement and the Sessional recommendation.

description: no mention of 6 month membership requirement for coming under care (repeated from previous year)
response: because of the above events, the Presbytery unanimously adopted a motion to wave the six-month membership requirement and the Sessional recommendation.

description: No examination in Bible content, yet man was licensed
response: Gulf Coast Presbytery respectfully disagrees with the exception of substance reported to the 25th General Assembly. The examination in question was an examination of a teaching elder from the Evangelical Presbyterian Church. The Book of Church Order only requires that teaching elders be examined in views of theology, sacraments, and church government (see BCO 13-6).

description: No directory or list of candidates or licentiates included (repeated from previous year)
response: M/S/C that Heritage Presbytery notes its oversight through its clerk for failure to provide complete rolls, and herein encloses them. The Clerk was ill with cancer. A volunteer did the rolls. They were completed, but not in time for the General Assembly.

description: Candidate's exception not explained.
response: Teaching Elder Erich Sauder made exception to WCF 218 which deals with the prohibition of any worldly recreation on the Sabbath he does not believe a total prohibition of physical and mind recreation is necessary to rest the human body and spirit on the Sabbath. He feels the confession does not adequately address the Sabbath rest issue as it applies to preparing of their hearts for rest from "Worldly Recreation."

description: minutes of commission to ordain and install not approved or attached
response: The Commission's report of Ordination and Installation is reported approved on page 388 Under "Care of the Eldership." The written report was not attached as an "Attachment" but will be added to these minutes.

description: parts of licensure examination not listed (repeated from previous year)
Parts of ordination examination not listed (repeated from previous year)
no indication of examination of a teaching elder coming into presbytery
there is no record of examination by the Presbytery, and no listing of the parts of examination
No obvious indication that the exam for ordination included each of the parts required by BCO 21-4 (repeated from previous year).
Steps of ordination exam need to be stated
No record of annual review of session records.
Presbytery required to meet twice a year. Minutes submitted for only one stated meeting.
Failure to examine a TE for reception into presbytery
Failure to sign the Statement of Ministerial Obligation.
No details of all parts of ordination examinations were given
No ruling elders named to commission (BCO 15-2). Only one ruling elder on commission (BCO 15-2) Repeated from last year.
No ruling elders named to commissions
response: Korean Southeastern Presbytery has recognized failure to comply with the provision of BCO, that stated, every commission shall include at least two Ruling Elders. We regret this irregularity, KSE Presbytery will commit to conform and be more careful in the future.

description: No quorum for presbytery meeting - only two ruling elders
response: KSE Presbytery fully acknowledges its various violations that breaches the regulation of BCO, its failure to report a quorum present, repeat this error again that we utilized. We humbly apologize for this inexcusable matter. We will do with our best effort and endeavor to prevent from repeating it in the future. KSE Presbytery will earnestly try to abide with the Constitution of our Denomination.

description: The entirety of minutes is taken up with a trial and discussion of whether to dismiss a church. However, on page 5, we learn that the hearing is postponed because the accused is not present. (BCO procedures for discipline need to be followed.)
no record of annual review of sessional records
No record that a commission was established to install a TE.
Parts of examination should be listed
no record of presbytery approval of session records (repeated from previous year)
Teaching elders transferred in and out of churches and presbytery without explanation or required examinations
Electing Ruling Elders requires training and congregational election and possible ordination
No record of congregational meetings to dissolve pastoral relationship. A TE was removed from Presbytery rolls without explanation.
Record of examinations for ordination and licensure is missing and parts of the exam are not recorded
Ruling elders should be examined by sessions and not by the Presbytery. NOTE: Though it is recognized that Korean Presbyteries are doing this in accord with their Korean customs and culture.
there is no record of presbytery's approval of session records; no record of commission to examine ruling elders before receiving mission church (BCO 13-8).
No standing rules submitted per RAO 14-4-c&e (second year).
no minutes of executive session
Expiration of license to preach Then licentiate did not apply for renewal. Procedures for renewal not followed.
Session records not reviewed in 1996.
Presbytery voted to sustain the examination of transferring PCA TE even though the TE stated that he had not fully read all of the PCA subordinate standards (BCO 21-5, questions 2 & 3).
response: 1. Mid-America Presbytery recognizes that the exception taken to the Minutes of the Called Meeting of 4 January 1997 taken by the 25th General Assembly is legitimate. 2. Presbytery respectfully directs the Assembly's attention to the Minutes of the 30th Stated Meeting of Mid-America Presbytery, where the TE in question indicated that he had fully read the subordinate standards of the PCA prior to his installation. 3. Presbytery has made it its practice since the Called Meeting of 4 January 1996 to fully inquire of all candidates for licensure, ordinands and ministers seeking admission to presbytery whether they have read the subordinate standards of the PCA, and to not proceed in sustaining their examinations at the Committee on Candidates and Credentials level or on the floor of presbytery unless and until they have complied with this requirement. 4. Presbytery makes its humble confession with regards to this matter, and promises, with God's help, to manifest its repentance by faithful adherence to the principles and requirements of our Church in this matter.

description: No record of exam in PCA church history for ordinand.
response: No Response: Presbytery is advised to respond to the 27th GA.

description: No actual minutes of stated meeting
response: Minutes were found and submitted for review.

description: There is no mention of sessional endorsement of a candidate under care or of the six-month membership requirement in a local congregation
response: The minutes indicate that the candidate was received in accordance with BCO 18-2 and 18-3.

description: Man licensed who "holds exception in his view of the sabbath (he does not believe that the Sabbath Day and Lord's Day are the same), the law (he is not absolutely sure that the Decalogue is the New Testament Ethic), and who may administer the Sacraments (he cannot see that the administration of the sacraments is the sole prerogative of teaching elders, he believes that ruling elders and others who have demonstrated knowledge, understanding, and maturity concerning the sacraments should be allowed to administer them.)"
response: Presbytery is currently in process of dealing with the licentiate and will respond to the 27th General Assembly.

description: Licentiate being examined for ordination was not examined in sacraments nor asked if his views have changed since licensure
No quorum for called meeting. No ruling elder present.
response: No response. Presbytery is advised to respond to the 27th GA.

description: No quorum for called meeting No ruling elder present.
response: No response. Presbytery is advised to respond to the 27th GA.

description: No record of completion and approval of internship in fulfillment of ordination requirements.
response: Regarding exceptions to the NTP Minutes of September 21, 1996 wherein Brad Freeman was examined for ordination and the SC omitted any reference to Mr. Freeman completing an intern program, the record should show that the files of the C&E Committee do contain adequate proof that Teaching Elder Freeman completed his internship in mississippi valley presbytery under the guidance of duncan Rankin. Teaching Elder Freeman's internship was subsequently approved by Mississippi Valley Presbytery.

description: Candidate examined in theology indicates a belief in continuing revelation. The minutes record no details of his views.
response: The sentence in 2410 which reads: "However, Candidate Dan wilensky had a guarded reservation regarding the issue of continuing revelation which was focused on heavily by the committee in their meeting" represents an editorial comment which should not have been included in the minutes of Presbytery's actions. The last sentence of paragraph 2410 makes this clear when it states that the Candidates and Examinations Committee is satisfied that Mr Wilensky's views are compatible with the PCA standards. Furthermore, Presbytery found Mr. Wilensky's views acceptable as attested by the action reported in 2410.3 wherein Mr. Wilensky's examination was sustained. The Presbytery deemed the candidate's views not to represent an exception to our standards The editorial comment raises a question where there should have been none; however, we failed as a Presbytery to catch this in our review and approval process. The North Texas Presbytery begs the pardon of the Assembly for the improper inclusion of this editorial comment in our minutes.

description: Presbytery approved a call that was not a proper call in that the call promised to provide financial care of the teaching elder yet also required the teaching elder to raise his financial support
response: Northeast Presbytery agrees with General Assembly's exception and promises to be more circumspect in the future.

description: Presbytery approved a man for membership who took exception to the standards on double predestination without recording the specifics of his differences.
response: Northeast Presbytery acknowledges that we erred in describing TE's views as exceptions TE submitted a letter stating his views which Presbytery judged not to be an exception to the confession. Presbytery therefore agrees with General Assembly's exception and promises to be more circumspect in the future.

description: Presbytery adopted a clarification of BCO 21-4 which removes the significance of the extraordinary Clause.
response: Presbytery agrees with the Exception and promises to be more careful in the future. The following Corrective Action was taken by Presbytery: Corrective Action: It was moved, seconded and passed by the presbytery of northern california at its Feb. 11 & 12, 1998 Presbytery Meeting the entire paragraph 14A (10) on page 8 of the Presbytery Minutes of March 1 & 2, 1996, which gives the "Clarification of the Exception Clause in BCO 21-4" adopted by the Presbytery at that meeting, be rescinded and that the Northern California Presbytery will comply with BCO 21-4.

description: Presbytery received into its teaching membership a TE who was unwilling to use the term "evangelical church" in fencing the table.
response: Presbytery agrees with the Exception and promises to be more careful in the future. The following Corrective Action was taken by Presbytery: Corrective Action: The Teaching Elder who took exception to using the term "evangelical church" in fencing the table withdrew his exception at the Feb. 11 & 12, 1998 Presbytery Meeting having come to a better understanding of BCO 58-4. It was then moved, seconded and passed by the presbytery of northern california that the entire paragraph 6A (2)(c) on page 3 of the presbytery minutes of june 15, 1996, which gives the Teaching Elder's exception, be rescinded.

description: presbytery approved the ordination of candidate whose views of sabbath permitting work and employment in labor other than deeds of necessity and mercy are contrary to PCA Standards
response: Presbytery agrees with the Exception and promises to be more careful in the future. The following Corrective Action was taken by Presbytery: Corrective Action: It was moved, seconded and passed by the presbytery of northern california at it's Feb. 11 & 12, 1998 Presbytery meeting to disallow the exception of substance to the PCA standards taken by a candidate for ordination at it's Oct. 11 & 12, 1996 Presbytery Meeting. presbytery reminded him of his vow of submission to the PCA Standards, that he may not teach his views which permit work and employment in labor other than deeds of necessity and mercy on the Sabbath, and recommended that he further study this issue. By this action the presbytery rescinded its approval of the exception to WCF XXI.7 taken by the candidate as given in paragraph 16B5b on page 7 of the October 11 & 12, 1996 Presbytery Minutes.

description: A Commission is established by Presbytery to discipline a church member without describing the reasons.
pastoral calls and appendices are not included; Minutes of January 23, 1993 are missing.
Presbytery took no action on TE's change of views.
response: Presbytery acknowledges the error. At its meeting in Edmonton, Alberta, October 3, 1997 the following recommendation was approved. Presbytery acknowledges TE's change of views, finds him still in "accord with. . .the fundamentals of [our] system of doctrine" (BCO 21-4, 2), and grants him full liberty to preach this exception. The recommendation was adopted. We trust this action will meet with your approval.

description: No record of dissolving Sioux Falls church or transferring or dismissing members
No record of installation of TE by presbytery
response: South Texas Presbytery admits its error and asks for forgiveness, but this error is a non-correctable error because since that time, South Texas Presbytery has dissolved the pastoral relationship of that individual.

description: Three churches given mission status without presbytery providing temporary government
response: South Texas Presbytery acknowledges its error and asks for forgiveness. Since July, 1996, Oakwood Community Church will provide the requirements for BCO 5-3 with the Natalia mission status, and the South Texas Presbytery Mission to North America Committee will designate fulfillment of BCO 5-3 for the Victoria mission.

Review of sessional minutes does not indicate whether they are approved or disapproved
response: We will take steps to correct this oversight.

description: Business other than that stated in the call should not have been conducted.
response: Presbytery was in error to transact business not stated in call for called meeting.

description: There is no record that the candidate's internship had been approved as required by BCO 21-4
response: Presbytery will be careful to record that a candidate's internship has been approved.

description: Review of sessional minutes does not indicate whether approved or disapproved
response: We will take steps to correct this oversight.

response: We will be careful to be more specific in recording these things

response: Presbytery will be careful to record that we have asked licentiates coming for ordination whether they have changed their view since licensure examination.

description: Session endorsements of the two candidates are not given as required by BCO 18-2 Also there is no mention that candidates have been members of the congregation whose session provides the endorsements for at least six months as required
response: We will be careful to be more specific in recording these things

description: Review of sessional minutes does not indicate whether approved or disapproved
response: We will take steps to correct this oversight.

description: Session endorsements of the two candidates are not given as required by BCO 18-2. Also there is no mention that candidates have been members of the congregation whose session provides the endorsements for at least six months as required
response: We will be careful to be more specific in recording these things.

description: There is no record of a congregational meeting of Woodland presbyterian church giving their agreement with the dissolution of the pastoral relation
response: Presbytery will be careful to record that the congregation has concurred in the Pastor's request for the dissolution of the pastoral relationship

description: Review of sessional minutes does not indicate whether approved or disapproved
response: We will take steps to correct this oversight.

description: The worship service for the purpose of organizing a church and installing its pastor included a lady who "led one congregation in the opening prayer" and another lady who "read passages of Scripture" prior to the sermon. (Commissioners Report MC-6). [N.B. - Southern Florida Presbytery submitted an inquiry rather than a response, as required by RAO 14-10.b, however, such action was not contumacious.] The 26th General Assembly refers Southern Florida Presbytery to 1 Corinthians 14:34; 1 Timothy 2:11-15; Westminster Larger Catechism Questions 155-159.
response: the presbytery of southern Florida, at its Stated Meeting held on October 21, 1997, voted to request from the Committee the grounds for the exception. the Rules of Assembly Operations 14-6 c. 1 define an exception as follows: Apparent violations of the Scripture or serious irregularities from the Constitution of the Presbyterian Church in America, out of accord with the deliverance's of the General Assembly, and matters of impropriety and important delinquencies. the review of presbytery records committee did not cite (sic) any authority for its opinion that our April 16, 1996 Minutes warranted an exception. When we receive your reply, Presbytery will make an appropriate response.

description: minutes of executive session not included
Minutes of Executive Session not included. (Note: this is the third year in a row that executive session minutes have not been submitted.)
No installation of pastor of newly merged church.
response: The Presbytery of the Southwest humbly acknowledges its error in not installing the pastor of the newly merged congregation. We are unable to correct the error due to changes in the pastorate of that church. TE has been properly installed as pastor, as testified by our April 1997 minutes.

description: Commission records were omitted.
No directory, List of Candidates and Licentiates. No Standing Rules. (Note this is the second year in a row for this exception)
No directory, roll of presbytery, list of candidates under care and licenciates, Standing Rules were included with the minutes.
Commission to ordain and install did not report as required by RAO 14-3.e.4.
response: The Stated Clerk failed to attach a copy of this report to the copies of the Fall Stated Meeting (October 3, 1996) submitted to the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records of the 25th GA. [NOTE: This was submitted and is included in materials for review at the 26th GA.]

description: Presbytery as a body has not taken action on the views of a TE regarding his belief in continuing revelation and in the continuation of all gifts of the Spirit (1 Corinthians 14, Romans 14, Ephesians 4) with the exception of apostleship (WCF 1-1 and BCO 13-9f). We recognize the extenuating circumstances which necessitated postponement at the July meeting.
response: The Court respectfully disagrees with this finding. Reason: According to BCO 13-9 a, it is the prerogative of the Presbytery to make such judgments as are in view in the exception stated above. Included within said prerogative would naturally be the determination of when such judgments should be made. Warrior Presbytery hears a report from its Membership Committee at each stated meeting regarding the Teaching Elder in question. He and the Committee are in frequent communication as he seeks to come to his position on the issues involved. He has honored his ordination vows by making known to his Presbytery the change in his views which are still in a state of transition. Presbytery continues to pray for and work with him in the hope that he will return with even greater conviction to the doctrines he subscribed to in his ordination. Presbytery feels that it is acting appropriately in this situation and believes that Presbyterian Polity in general and the BCO in particular leaves such determinations exclusively to the Presbytery.
